question: How do you approach learning complex CrossFit movements like the jerk?
answer: I found that instead of trying to add the jumping motion into the overhead press all at once, I needed to break it down. I practiced just jumping with the bar at my shoulders to feel the weight becoming weightless as it travels with my body. This helped me understand when to start pushing the bar overhead, making the movement less awkward and more manageable.

question: What challenges do you face with on-call shifts and how do you manage rest?
answer: My recent on-call shifts were brutal because my phone kept waking me up every 15 minutes with non-urgent emails, disrupting my sleep. To manage this, I decided to take the morning off work to catch up on rest. I also communicate with my team about taking time off when needed, especially after stressful on-call periods, to ensure proper recovery.

question: Why do you think people don't take enough time off work despite having discretionary time off policies?
answer: In my experience, many people don't take enough time off even when policies allow it because they either feel guilty or don't prioritize rest. I believe it's important for management and leadership to actively remind and encourage people to take their time off. Otherwise, employees might go months without vacation, which isn't healthy or sustainable.
